summary: Nimbalyst (github.com/nimbalyst/nimbalyst) is a free, local-first Electron desktop app ("visual workspace for building with Codex and Claude Code") that runs multiple AI coding agent sessions in parallel with a session kanban board, task trackers, git worktree isolation, WYSIWYG diff review, and an iOS/Android companion app for remote control. It supports Claude Code (two billing paths: Agent SDK API-billed + genuine `claude` CLI subscription-billed in a PTY), OpenAI Codex (three transports: app-server JSON-RPC, codex-sdk exec, and ACP), OpenCode, and GitHub Copilot CLI. All agent execution is local on the desktop; mobile is a remote-control relay through wss://sync.nimbalyst.com, not cloud execution.
tech_stack: TypeScript/Electron monorepo with npm workspaces. Renderer: React + Jotai atoms (packages/electron/src/renderer/store/atoms/) + Tailwind + Lexical/Monaco editors; embedded terminal via ghostty-web ^0.4.0 + node-pty ^1.1.0. Main process: Electron with PGLite (PostgreSQL-in-WASM, @electric-sql/pglite 0.3.14) in a worker thread for persistence (~/Library/Application Support/@nimbalyst/electron/pglite-db/), with an in-progress migration to better-sqlite3 ^12.10.0 (packages/electron/src/main/database/sqlite/PGLiteToSQLiteMigrator.ts). Agent SDKs: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk ^0.3.161, @openai/codex-sdk ^0.136.0, @agentclientprotocol/sdk, @opencode-ai/sdk, @zed-industries/codex-acp. Git via simple-git; GitHub via the `gh` CLI (`gh api`). iOS app in SwiftUI (packages/ios), Android (packages/android). Cross-platform runtime services in packages/runtime.
architecture: Three layers. (1) packages/runtime/src/ai/server/ is the provider/protocol core: BaseAgentProvider subclasses (ClaudeCodeProvider.ts 3304 lines, OpenAICodexProvider.ts 2866 lines, OpenAICodexACPProvider.ts, CopilotCLIProvider.ts, OpenCodeProvider.ts, ClaudeCodeCliProvider.ts) sit on top of normalized AgentProtocol adapters (protocols/ProtocolInterface.ts defines createSession/resumeSession/forkSession/sendMessage->AsyncIterable<ProtocolEvent>/abortSession): ClaudeSDKProtocol, CodexAppServerProtocol, CodexSDKProtocol, CodexACPProtocol, CopilotACPProtocol, OpenCodeSDKProtocol. Every protocol emits normalized ProtocolEvents (text, reasoning, tool_call, tool_result, usage, raw_event, error, complete). ProviderFactory.ts + ModelRegistry.ts route `provider:model` identifiers (ModelIdentifier.ts). (2) packages/electron/src/main/ hosts services: ai/AIService.ts + ai/MessageStreamingHandler.ts consume provider streams, persist raw events to ai_agent_messages, drive SessionStateManager; MetaAgentService.ts (orchestrator agent spawning child sessions via an MCP server, mcp/metaAgentServer.ts), SuperLoopService.ts (Ralph-style iteration loops), BlitzHandlers.ts (parallel multi-model runs), GitWorktreeService.ts + WorktreeStore.ts, TerminalSessionManager.ts (PTYs), SyncManager.ts (mobile relay), PullRequestHandlers.ts (gh CLI). A loopback HTTP MCP server ("nimbalyst-mcp") with per-session `&sessionId=` URLs + bearer tokens exposes Nimbalyst tools (tracker CRUD, AskUserQuestion, git commit proposal, editor tools — main/mcp/tools/). (3) Renderer UI: AgentMode (session manager), TrackerMode (KanbanBoard.tsx for tracker items, SessionKanbanBoard.tsx for sessions), transcript rendering driven by transcript parsers (runtime/src/ai/server/transcript/: TranscriptTransformer.ts, CodexRawParser.ts, CodexAppServerRawParser.ts, ClaudeCodeTranscriptAdapter) that re-project persisted raw events into a canonical transcript. Data flow: renderer IPC -> AIService.sendMessage -> provider -> protocol child process/SDK -> StreamChunks back over IPC + DB writes + SessionStateManager events broadcast to all windows and the mobile sync layer.
claude_code_integration: Two distinct Claude Code paths. PATH A (default, provider id `claude-code`, API-key or OAuth billed): in-process use of @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk `query()` — no manual CLI spawn; the SDK spawns the native claude binary itself. packages/runtime/src/ai/server/providers/ClaudeCodeProvider.ts:732: `const leadQuery: AsyncIterable<any> = query({ prompt: promptInput as any, options });`. Options are built in providers/claudeCode/sdkOptionsBuilder.ts: `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable: effectivePath` (resolved from the SDK's per-platform native binary packages, asar-unpacked, via claudeCode/cliPathResolver.ts -> runtime/src/electron/claudeCodeEnvironment.ts), `systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code', append: systemPrompt }` (or plain string for meta-agent), `settingSources: ['user','project','local']`, `mcpServers` (per-session nimbalyst-mcp config), `permissionMode` mapped planning->'plan', auto->'auto', else 'default', `canUseTool` callback for permission prompts, `hooks: { PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionDenied }`, `resume: claudeSessionId` + `forkSession: true` for branch-from-session, `model` (variant like 'opus'), env with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY stripped from all sources, `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH: 'auto:2'`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT: 'cli'` (deliberately set so Anthropic's backend classifies traffic as first-party CLI instead of throttled 'sdk-ts'), `CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL` from per-session effort setting, and agent-team env (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS / CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS / CLAUDE_CODE_TEAM_NAME / CLAUDE_CODE_AGENT_ID). Critically, the prompt is always a persistent AsyncIterable (createPersistentPromptStream) — never a bare string — so the SDK does not close the binary's stdin on `type:'result'`; a 30s grace timer (PROMPT_GRACE_MS = 30_000 in ClaudeCodeProvider.ts) ends the stream, fixing "Tool permission request failed: Error: Stream closed" on late can_use_tool requests. PATH B (provider id `claude-code-cli`, Pro/Max subscription, NIM-806): spawns the genuine interactive `claude` CLI in a ghostty-web PTY terminal strip. packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/claudeCliSpawnConfig.ts builds the argv: `--model <opus|sonnet|haiku>`, `--mcp-config <tempfile>.mcp.json`, `--resume <id>` or `--continue` or `--session-id <ulid>` (Nimbalyst pre-allocates the session id; switches to --resume if the jsonl already exists per claudeCliJsonlPath.ts), `--dangerously-skip-permissions` (only when workspace permission mode is allow-all/bypass-all), `--settings <json>` registering a PreToolUse permission hook that POSTs to a loopback /permission endpoint (claudeCliPermissionHookConfig.ts), `--add-dir <dirs>`, `--allowedTools mcp__<server> ...`, `--disallowedTools ...`, `--append-system-prompt <nudge>` — explicitly "never -p" (interactive, not headless). ClaudeCliSessionLauncher.ts orchestrates: write per-session MCP config to os.tmpdir()/nimbalyst-claude-cli/<id>.mcp.json, optionally start a loopback proxy and set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL at it for transcript observation, strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and the nested-session CLAUDECODE env var, then TerminalSessionManager.createClaudeCliTerminal spawns it under node-pty. Observation backends reconstruct the transcript: proxy (claudeCliObservation/claudeApiProxy.ts intercepts /v1/messages SSE), jsonl tailing of ~/.claude/projects/.../<session>.jsonl, or terminal-only; claudeCliSubAgentTracker.ts re-attributes Task sub-agent traffic on the proxy wire (no parent_tool_use_id there).
codex_integration: Provider `openai-codex` (OpenAICodexProvider.ts) with selectable transport (`aiProviders.openai-codex.transport` setting, type CodexTransport = 'sdk' | 'app-server', default app-server in production). TRANSPORT 1 (default), protocols/CodexAppServerProtocol.ts: spawns one long-lived child per session — `spawn(binary, ['app-server', '--listen', 'stdio://'], { env, cwd, stdio: ['pipe','pipe','pipe'] })` (line 400) — and speaks JSON-RPC v2 over newline-delimited JSON (codexAppServer/jsonRpcClient.ts). Lifecycle: `initialize` (capabilities {experimentalApi:true}) -> `thread/start` with ThreadStartParams { model, sandbox: permissionMode==='bypass-all' ? 'danger-full-access' : 'workspace-write', cwd, approvalPolicy: 'never', developerInstructions: systemPrompt, config: { ...codexConfigOverrides, model_reasoning_effort } } -> per turn `turn/start { threadId, input }`; resume = fresh spawn + `thread/resume` forwarding the full config (fallback thread/start); abort via `turn/interrupt`. Effort mapping: Nimbalyst 'max' -> codex 'xhigh', else passthrough (default 'high'). Server->client approval RPCs (item/fileChange/requestApproval, item/commandExecution/requestApproval, applyPatchApproval, execCommandApproval, item/tool/call dynamic tools) are routed to host bindings that hit Nimbalyst's permission UI. Notifications (item/started, item/completed, item/agentMessage/delta, item/reasoning/textDelta, thread/tokenUsage/updated, turn/completed, turn/failed) are translated to ProtocolEvents; fileChange items carry full unified diffs which are reverse-applied (codex/patchReverse.ts) to recover pre-edit baselines — the stated reason this transport replaced the SDK. CODEX_API_KEY env set when an API key is configured; vendored rg helper dir is prepended to PATH (codexAppServerBinary.ts getCodexVendorPathEntries). Binary resolved from @openai/codex platform packages (vendor/<triple>/bin/codex) or packaged Electron resources. TRANSPORT 2 (legacy escape hatch), protocols/CodexSDKProtocol.ts: @openai/codex-sdk `new Codex({ apiKey, codexPathOverride, config, env })`, `client.startThread({ model: options.model || 'gpt-5', workingDirectory, skipGitRepoCheck: true, approvalPolicy: 'never', sandboxMode, modelReasoningEffort, developer_instructions, additionalDirectories })` then `thread.runStreamed(input)` — the SDK spawns `codex exec --experimental-json` per turn and forwards `config` as dotted-path `--config` TOML flags and additionalDirectories as repeated `--add-dir`. OpenAICodexProvider.buildCodexConfigOverrides (line ~1873) injects `show_raw_agent_reasoning: true`, `mcp_servers` (TOML-bare-key-sanitized names), and a codex `hooks.PreToolUse` matcher `^apply_patch$` running `"${process.execPath}" "${hookScriptPath}"` under ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE to snapshot pre-edit file content before codex writes. TRANSPORT 3 (separate provider `openai-codex-acp`, OpenAICodexACPProvider.ts + protocols/CodexACPProtocol.ts): spawns the @zed-industries/codex-acp native binary (command override via NIMBALYST_CODEX_ACP_COMMAND/NIMBALYST_CODEX_ACP_ARGS) and speaks ACP via @agentclientprotocol/sdk ndJsonStream over stdio — chosen because ACP exposes writeTextFile client callbacks (native pre/post-edit hooks). Auth: CodexAuthService.ts / CodexAuthHandlers.ts manage ChatGPT-login vs API key; the app-server transport does a pre-flight account check before first turn.
status_detection: Multi-mechanism, centralized in packages/runtime/src/ai/server/SessionStateManager.ts (EventEmitter + DB sync). Status enum: 'idle' | 'running' | 'waiting_for_input' | 'error' (+ interrupted event), persisted in ai_sessions.status and broadcast as session:started/streaming/waiting/completed/error/interrupted events to all windows, tray, mobile sync, and MetaAgentService. Sources of truth: (1) Streaming loop — MessageStreamingHandler.ts (packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/) flips status to 'waiting_for_input' when a canUseTool permission request, AskUserQuestion, or ExitPlanMode prompt is pending (lines ~712-841) and back to 'running' when resolved; 'error' on stream failure; endSession->'idle' emits session:completed. (2) Protocol completion events — every protocol's sendMessage generator yields a terminal `complete`/`error` ProtocolEvent (Claude SDK `type:'result'` chunk; codex `turn/completed`/`turn/failed` JSON-RPC notifications; exit codes/stderr tails appended to errors via appendStderrTail in CodexAppServerProtocol.ts). (3) PID-file polling for the subscription CLI path — claudeCliPidState.ts polls `~/.claude/sessions/{pid}.json` (the file `claude ps` reads) mapping busy/idle/waiting -> running/idle/waiting_for_input, with staleness detection; deliberately preferred over jsonl tailing or TUI scraping. (4) Transcript observation — loopback ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL proxy parsing SSE (claudeApiProxy.ts) and/or jsonl file tailing reconstruct what the CLI is doing. (5) File watching — HooklessAgentFileWatcher.ts runs a chokidar SessionFileWatcher + FileSnapshotCache per session to attribute file edits when no tool event is available (Bash edits, codex apply_patch races). (6) Crash recovery — SessionStateManager.recoverStaleSessions() on boot marks 'running' rows idle (2-minute stale threshold) but deliberately preserves 'waiting_for_input' rows. Race protection: post-DB-write check skips emitting stale waiting events superseded by running.
task_and_board_model: Two boards. (A) SESSION KANBAN (renderer/components/TrackerMode/SessionKanbanBoard.tsx + store/atoms/sessionKanban.ts): columns are fixed phases — 'backlog' | 'planning' | 'implementing' | 'validating' | 'complete' (+ an "unphased" column) — stored as `metadata.phase` JSONB on each ai_sessions row; drag-drop calls setPhase per session (handleDrop, SessionKanbanBoard.tsx:1570). Cards are sessions, workstreams, or worktrees (getCardType: meta.worktreeId -> 'worktree', sessionType==='workstream' -> 'workstream'); workstream cards derive their phase from the most-active child (PHASE_PRIORITY) and show a ChildRunStateSummary {running, waiting, review, idle, done}. Cmd+Left/Right moves cards between phases. Moving a card does NOT trigger agent runs — phase is purely organizational metadata; execution is driven by sending/queueing prompts. (B) TRACKER KANBAN (KanbanBoard.tsx): task/bug/decision items defined by YAML tracker configs (.nimbalyst/trackers/*.yaml) stored as markdown-backed TrackerRecords with status field (to-do -> in-progress -> in-review...), fractional-index ordering (generateKeyBetween), and agent-writable via MCP tools tracker_create/tracker_list/tracker_update/tracker_link_session (main/mcp/tools/trackerToolHandlers.ts; workflow conventions in docs/TRACKER_WORKFLOWS.md). PERSISTENCE: PGLite tables — ai_sessions (id, workspace_id, provider, model, title, session_type 'session'|'workstream'|'blitz', parent_session_id, worktree_id, status, provider_session_id, draft_input, metadata JSONB), ai_agent_messages (full raw transcript), session_files (edited/referenced/read links), worktrees (id, name adjective-noun, path, branch, base_branch), super_loops/super_iterations, queued_prompts, tracker_items index. Session hierarchy invariant (docs/SESSION_HIERARCHY.md): worktree IS the workstream; worktree sessions have parent_session_id NULL. Queued prompts: prompts queued against a busy session are claimed and dispatched on idle (queuedPromptDispatcher.ts).
review_loop: No dedicated cross-agent implement->review->fix pipeline, but three adjacent mechanisms: (1) MetaAgentService.ts — any session can spawn child sessions (optionally in fresh worktrees, optionally inheriting or overriding model/provider) via MCP tools; the service subscribes to SessionStateManager and on child session:completed/error/waiting/interrupted queues a "[Child Session Update]" notification prompt (with child title, status, last response, edited files, pending prompt) back into the PARENT session's prompt queue (handleChildSessionEvent, line ~866) — this is the feedback-routing mechanism: the parent agent reads the child's result and can re-prompt or fix. Fire-and-forget via metadata.notifyParent=false; spurious completions suppressed while the child still has queued prompts. (2) Super Loops (SuperLoopService.ts, shared/types/superLoop.ts) — Ralph-loop-style: planning phase writes IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, then each building iteration is a brand-new fresh-context session in the same worktree; the agent must call the `super_loop_progress_update` MCP tool (mcp/superLoopProgressServer.ts) writing .superloop/progress.json {phase, status, completionSignal, learnings, blockers}; the service checks exit conditions per iteration (completed/blocked/max_iterations/consecutive-failures) — self-review via persistent files + git history rather than a second reviewer agent. (3) Blitz (BlitzHandlers.ts) — same prompt fanned out to up to 10 worktrees with per-worktree model assignments (e.g. 2x claude-code:opus + 2x openai-codex:gpt-5.x); when all children complete, an analysis session is auto-created (default model claude-code:opus-1m, atomic jsonb_set guard) whose prompt instructs it to compare all child sessions via session-context MCP tools and recommend a winner — a judge/review agent over parallel implementations. Human review is first-class instead: red/green pending-diff approval per file edit (pre-edit baselines captured per transport), plus the 'validating' kanban phase and 'in-review' tracker status as manual states.
worktree_and_git: GitWorktreeService.ts (simple-git): createWorktree generates an adjective-noun name ('swift-falcon'), branch `worktree/<name>`, directory `../<project>_worktrees/<name>`, base branch defaulting to repo's current branch; status reporting includes commitsAhead/Behind, patch-equivalence merge detection, uncommitted-change counts; deleteWorktree removes dir + registration + branch. Worktree rows in the `worktrees` table; ai_sessions.worktree_id FK (ON DELETE SET NULL preserves history). One worktree : many sessions; the worktree acts as the workstream container in the UI. Orchestrator/codex sessions get sibling worktrees + project root passed as additionalDirectories -> codex `--add-dir` / claude additionalDirectories so cross-worktree edits pass the sandbox. GitRefWatcher (main/file/GitRefWatcher.ts) watches refs for status refresh. PRs: PullRequestHandlers.ts + GhApiService.ts shell out to `gh api` for PR fetching/caching with per-project gh account selection (`gh auth status` / `gh auth switch` hints on 404); AI git commit via an MCP `developer_git_commit_proposal` tool rendering a commit-confirmation widget (gitCommitProposalPromptUtils.ts). Blitz archives losing worktrees via archiveWorktree.
remote_cloud: No cloud agent execution — all agent processes run on the user's desktop. A separate sync server at wss://sync.nimbalyst.com (closed-source) relays Yjs-style collaboration + session state to the iOS/Android apps (packages/runtime/src/sync/: ProjectSyncProvider.ts, TrackerSyncEngine.ts, collabHistoryClient.ts, syncContentTruncator.ts; wire types in packages/collab-protocol). Mobile devices send SessionControlMessage payloads ('prompt', 'prompt_response', 'cancel', 'archive') which MobileSessionControlHandler.ts (packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/) interprets and dispatches into the local AI session logic — so a phone can answer an AskUserQuestion, approve a tool permission, respond to a commit proposal, or queue the next prompt, and the desktop agent resumes immediately. Push notifications tell the user when agents need attention.
key_files: /tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/providers/ClaudeCodeProvider.ts — main Claude Code provider; claude-agent-sdk query() streaming loop, 30s stdin grace timer, teammate orchestration

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/providers/claudeCode/sdkOptionsBuilder.ts — builds every SDK option: binary path, permissionMode mapping, hooks, sanitized env, resume/forkSession, persistent AsyncIterable prompt

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/protocols/CodexAppServerProtocol.ts — spawn(binary, ['app-server','--listen','stdio://']) + JSON-RPC v2 thread/turn lifecycle, approval RPC routing, diff reverse-apply baselines

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/protocols/CodexSDKProtocol.ts — legacy @openai/codex-sdk transport (codex exec --experimental-json per turn), sandboxMode/modelReasoningEffort mapping
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/providers/OpenAICodexProvider.ts — codex provider: transport selection, MCP->TOML config overrides, apply_patch PreToolUse snapshot hook
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/protocols/CodexACPProtocol.ts — codex via @zed-industries/codex-acp over ACP stdio (native writeTextFile pre-edit hooks)

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/claudeCliSpawnConfig.ts — exact argv builder for the genuine `claude` CLI (--model/--mcp-config/--resume/--session-id/--settings/--add-dir/--allowedTools, never -p)

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/ClaudeCliSessionLauncher.ts — pre-allocated session id, temp MCP config, permission hook, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL observation proxy, PTY spawn
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/claudeCliPidState.ts — polls ~/.claude/sessions/{pid}.json for busy/idle/waiting turn state
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/SessionStateManager.ts — central status state machine (running/waiting_for_input/idle/error), DB sync, stale-session recovery
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/ai/MessageStreamingHandler.ts — consumes provider streams, flips waiting_for_input on permission/question prompts, file tracking
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/renderer/store/atoms/sessionKanban.ts — phase columns (backlog/planning/implementing/validating/complete), card types, child run-state rollups

/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/SuperLoopService.ts — fresh-context iteration loop with progress.json MCP exit conditions
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/ipc/BlitzHandlers.ts — same prompt across N worktrees with per-worktree models + auto analysis/judge session
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/electron/src/main/services/GitWorktreeService.ts — simple-git worktree create/status/delete, adjective-noun naming, worktree/ branch prefix
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/protocols/OpenCodeSDKProtocol.ts — spawn('opencode', ['serve','--port',N]) HTTP+SSE singleton server
/tmp/friday-research/nimbalyst/packages/runtime/src/ai/server/protocols/CopilotACPProtocol.ts — spawn copilot --acp --stdio JSON-RPC

other_agents: Beyond Claude Code and Codex: OpenCode (alpha) — OpenCodeSDKProtocol.ts spawns a singleton `opencode serve --port <free-port>` HTTP+SSE server with reference counting, talks @opencode-ai/sdk client; GitHub Copilot CLI (alpha) — CopilotACPProtocol.ts spawns `copilot --acp --stdio` and speaks ACP JSON-RPC (auth errors surfaced as 'run copilot and /login'). The abstraction is two-tier: (1) AgentProtocol interface (protocols/ProtocolInterface.ts) — platform string, createSession/resumeSession/forkSession returning ProtocolSession {id, platform, raw}, sendMessage(session, message) -> AsyncIterable<ProtocolEvent> with a closed event vocabulary (text/reasoning/tool_call/tool_result/usage/raw_event/planning_mode_entered/exited/error/complete), abortSession/cleanupSession; (2) BaseAgentProvider subclasses add Nimbalyst concerns: permission mixin (ProviderPermissionMixin.ts), MCP config injection (McpConfigService), system prompt construction, session-id mapping (ProviderSessionManager), transcript adapters. Tool-call names are normalized cross-transport (e.g. codex MCP calls renamed to Claude-style `mcp__<server>__<tool>`, codex fileChange renamed 'file_change') so one renderer/widget layer serves all agents. Every raw SDK/protocol event is also persisted via 'raw_event' so per-transport parsers (CodexRawParser/CodexAppServerRawParser/CodexRawParserDispatcher) can re-project transcripts after the fact. Providers are also split by BILLING path as separate provider ids (claude-code vs claude-code-cli; openai-codex vs openai-codex-acp) with a session-level lock preventing switching between API-billed and subscription-billed paths mid-session. Slash commands/skills are unified by AgentWorkflowService.ts which exports workspace .claude/commands and skills to provider-native formats (claude vs codex-style).
takeaways: Patterns worth stealing for a kanban app orchestrating Claude Code + Codex with per-column model defaults and an implement->review loop: (1) Protocol-adapter layer: define one AgentProtocol interface (createSession/resume/fork + sendMessage -> AsyncIterable of normalized events incl. a raw_event passthrough for lossless persistence) and put ALL CLI quirks in adapters — Nimbalyst supports 6 transports behind one streaming loop and one transcript renderer. (2) Use the official SDKs, not hand-rolled stream-json parsing: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk query() (with pathToClaudeCodeExecutable for packaged builds, canUseTool callback for in-app permission UI, resume/forkSession, hooks) and codex `app-server --listen stdio://` JSON-RPC (persistent per-session child, thread/start + turn/start, approvalPolicy:'never' with approval RPCs routed to your UI) — codex app-server beats `codex exec --experimental-json` because fileChange notifications carry full unified diffs for pre-edit baselines. (3) Model/effort mapping: store `provider:model` identifiers; map your effort scale to codex model_reasoning_effort ('max'->'xhigh') and Claude's CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL env; set CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT='cli' to avoid sdk-ts rate-limit deprioritization; always strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY from inherited env to avoid surprise billing. (4) Status: drive a small state machine (running/waiting_for_input/idle/error) from stream events + permission-prompt lifecycle, persist to DB, recover stale 'running' rows on boot but never clobber 'waiting_for_input'; keep a persistent stdin stream + grace timer for Claude's late permission requests. (5) Kanban-as-metadata: store phase on the session row (metadata.phase JSONB) and keep columns organizational, with worktree==workstream cards rolling up child run states — per-column model defaults would slot naturally into the column definition where Nimbalyst has only colors. (6) Implement->review loop: Nimbalyst's strongest primitive is MetaAgentService's child-completion -> queue a structured '[Child Session Update]' prompt (status, last response, edited files) into the parent's queue — generalize that into implementer-completes -> spawn reviewer session (different provider/model) in the same worktree -> reviewer output queued back to implementer; Blitz's auto-created analysis/judge session (atomic jsonb_set guard against double-creation) is the exact template for an automated review column. (7) One worktree per task card (adjective-noun name, worktree/<name> branch, ../<project>_worktrees/ dir) with sessions FK'd to it, --add-dir/additional_writable_roots for cross-worktree orchestrators, and gh CLI (not octokit) for PR ops. (8) Inject a per-session loopback MCP server URL (&sessionId= + bearer token) into every agent so agent tool calls (task updates, questions, commit proposals) land in the right card/transcript regardless of which CLI produced them.
